steps.title: Spot the explicit consumption rate
steps.content: The passage directly states the bowhead whale consumes “19,800 kg of krill per day”—a specific figure we can use to compare against the sei whale's consumption capacity.

steps.title: Compare the consumption rates
steps.content: The sei whale's maximum consumption is 30% of 25,000 kg, or 7,500 kg per day. The bowhead consumes up to 19,800 kg per day, so it eats significantly more. Choice C states exactly this relationship. The others either deny sei whales eat krill (A), wrongly equate both whales' consumption (B), or fabricate food competition issues (D). That matches choice C — C is correct.
